subject: Add support for TP-Link UB500 Adapter (#33)
author: birdybro

impact.effect_if_absent: TP-Link UB500 Adapter (RTL8761B) will not be recognized/supported

notes:
Backport from upstream v5.16 (commit 4fd6d490796171bf786090fee782e252186632e4, authored by Nicholas Flintham, 2021-10-01) to MiSTer's v5.15.1 base; fork commit dated 2022-07-20 postdates vanilla by ~9.5 months, consistent with `is_backport_of_vanilla=true`. The VID:PID 0x2357:0x0604 is supported in 6.18.38 with identical code (BTUSB_REALTEK | BTUSB_WIDEBAND_SPEECH flags) at btusb.c:787-788, byte-identical to the fork's addition. Comment in 6.18.38 reads 'Additional Realtek 8761BUV' (BUV variant) vs MiSTer's 'Additional Realtek 8761B' (B variant) — device ID is same, comment evolution only. No userspace coupling found in Main_MiSTer. Patch-provenance.md (line 343) correctly documents this as upstream in 6.18, exact match.

sonnet-verified: re-derived vanilla SHA independently via `git log` in /mnt/source/linux (confirmed ancestor of v6.18.38), re-grepped btusb.c:787-788 and confirmed exact quote match, cross-checked commit dates confirming true backport direction, re-derived device ID 2357:0604 against the live diff. Only change made: expanded the vanilla SHA from a 12-char abbreviation to the full 40-char form for precision. No factual errors found.

sonnet-verified: confirmed
